overview: Azorius Stoneblade is a Legacy control deck that leverages White and Blue's superior card selection and disruption to outlast opponents. It uses Stoneforge Mystic to fetch a stabilizing sword, Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student for massive card advantage, and Stifle to shut down key opponent spells. The goal is to establish a resilient board state and win through incremental value and targeted interaction.
win_conditions: Stoneforge Mystic fetching a sword to stabilize and generate value
Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student drawing into answers and outlasting the opponent
Stifle countering game-winning combo pieces or tutors to stall for time

keep_criteria: Hands containing Stifle or other early interaction
Two or more lands with a path to Stoneforge Mystic or Tamiyo
Hands that can respond to common Legacy opening plays

Keep hands with 2-3 lands (prioritize basics or fetchable duals), a turn-1 land, and at least one of: Stoneforge Mystic, Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student, Stifle, or efficient interaction (Path to Exile, Swords to Plowshares, Counterspell).
Prioritize keeping hands that can play a land and a spell on turn 1, or two lands and a spell on turn 2.
mulligan_criteria: Hands with no interaction or removal
Hands with three or more lands
Hands lacking a clear path to the sword or card advantage engine
Mulligan hands with 0-1 lands, 3+ lands with no spells, or hands consisting solely of counterspells/removal without threats or Stoneforge.

early_game: Prioritize playing lands and holding up mana for Stifle
Use Stifle to counter fetch lands, tutors, or early combo pieces
Play Stoneforge Mystic as early as possible to set up the sword
Turn 1: Play a land, cast a cantrip (Opt/Opt) or cheap interaction if threatened, or play a 1-drop if available.
Turn 2: Play a second land, then cast Stoneforge Mystic or Tamiyo if possible.
If opponent plays a threat, use spot removal or counterspell to stabilize.
If they pass, develop board with Tamiyo or equip Sword of Feast and Famine to a threat.
Land sequencing: Prioritize fetching/finding basic Plains/Islands early to ensure consistent casting of WU spells
mid_game: Fetch the designated sword using Stoneforge Mystic
Cast Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student to draw cards and find answers
Maintain a hand of interaction while developing board presence
Preserve counterspells for key combo pieces or game-winning threats
use spot removal on early pressure.
Tamiyo’s loyalty should be spent on card draw (+1) when ahead, or exile/removal (-2/-3) when behind or facing problematic permanents.
Sword of Feast and Famine is a win condition and removal tool—equip it to your best threat or use it to exile a key permanent.
Don't overcommit mana into Tamiyo if you can't protect it
keep a counterspell or Stifle in hand as insurance.
late_game: Stabilize with the sword's defensive or value-generating abilities
Use Tamiyo's draw to out-resource the opponent
Close the game once the opponent runs out of threats or interaction

Sword of Feast and Famine equipped to a 4/4 or larger threat, Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student (especially with +1 card draw to find the sword or a finisher), and a well-timed Stifle to protect your win condition.
Sometimes a single Sword of Feast and Famine on a 3/3 with trample/first strike is enough to close out games.
Go all-in when you have a Sword equipped, Tamiyo on the board, and at least one counterspell or Stifle to protect your board.
Also commit when the opponent has no removal or sweepers in hand and you're ahead in life.
If you've stabilized and have a clear path to damage, stop holding back and start attacking with equipped threats.

key_cards.name: Stoneforge Mystic
key_cards.notes: Play on curve and equip immediately to stabilize against early aggression or set up late-game value.

key_cards.name: Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student
key_cards.notes: Use its draw ability to refill your hand after interaction, and its tutor ability to find specific answers or the sword if needed.

key_cards.name: Stifle
key_cards.notes: Hold up mana to counter spells with mana costs, particularly fetch lands, tutors, or combo finishers, to disrupt the opponent's game plan.

sequencing_tips: Always hold up Stifle until you know what the opponent is casting
Play Stoneforge Mystic before casting Tamiyo to ensure the sword is available for defense
Use Tamiyo's draw ability when your hand is low and you need specific answers
Manage mana carefully to keep interaction available while developing board presence
Common mistakes include overplaying Tamiyo without protection, using Sword of Feast and Famine as mere removal instead of a win condition, and hoarding counterspells until it's too late.
Remember that Stoneforge Mystic is a 3-mana 2/2—don't attack with it unless you're sure it's safe.
Tamiyo's -2 ability can exile your own threats if you're not careful
Stifle is a 1-mana instant—use it to counter key spells rather than just combo pieces.
Finally, this deck thrives on patience

matchups.advice: Use Stifle aggressively on tutors, fetch lands, or key combo pieces to buy time for Stoneforge and Tamiyo.
Prioritize early Stifle on threats if possible, but focus on playing Stoneforge quickly to fetch a defensive sword.
Race to establish the sword and use Tamiyo to draw out their interaction while holding Stifle for their win conditions.
Disrupt their early game with Stifle, then stabilize with the sword and outdraw them with Tamiyo.
Stifle their mana acceleration or tutors, then race to the sword before they assemble a large threat.

Use Stifle to counter their counterspells or key spells. Cast threats at end step or when they have no mana. Use Tamiyo's +1 to draw into answers or finishers. Don't overcommit to a single threat; attack with multiple small threats if possible.

Win the card advantage war. Use Tamiyo to draw into more cards. Stifle their counterspells or key spells. Prioritize keeping Sword of Feast and Famine in play. Use spot removal efficiently and don't overcommit to a single threat. Track their hand and use counterspells to protect your win conditions.

common_threats: Watch for combo decks playing Show and Tell, Delver of Secrets, or Arcane Signet/Chalice of the Void.
Track opponent's hand size and discard effects
if they've Thoughtseized or Duress, expect a specific game plan.
Note when they play cheap interaction—they're likely setting up a midgame threat or combo.
Pay attention to Tamiyo's exile zone
